retry_reason: Failure occurs only when creation (br_vlan_add) fails, typically due to memory allocation or invalid parameters; retrying is not standard and the caller should propagate the error.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for a master VLAN object. Returns a pointer to the master VLAN with an acquired reference (refcount incremented or initialized to 1). The caller must release this reference with a corresponding put (e.g., br_vlan_put) when done. If the function returns NULL, the object was not obtained and the caller must not use it. The function may create a new VLAN entry if one does not exist, and on failure it returns NULL with an extack error message.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to get-or-create a master VLAN object and manage its refcount (either set to 1 for new or increment for existing). The only additional logic is the lookup and creation, which are integral to the get-or-create pattern.
judge.retry_reason: Failure occurs only when creation (br_vlan_add) fails, typically due to memory allocation or invalid parameters; retrying is not standard and the caller should propagate the error.
judge.contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for a master VLAN object. Returns a pointer to the master VLAN with an acquired reference (refcount incremented or initialized to 1). check.reason: The function returns a pointer to masterv (net_bridge_vlan). When a new VLAN is created, its refcount is set to 1 via refcount_set; when found, its refcount is incremented via refcount_inc. Both operations act on the same return value and access path masterv.refcnt.refs. The type chain matches the callee info and struct definitions. Segment counts (3) are equal.
